tool_input.summary: Identified MER Spirit (MER2) in-situ datasets most directly supporting the interpretation of the Home Plate high-silica outcrops as opaline-silica sinter (hot spring/geyser deposits): Pancam/MI imaging for sinter-like textures and geomorphic context; Mini-TES spectroscopy for opaline silica identification; APXS chemistry for extremely high SiO2 and low other oxides; MB spectra as supporting mineralogical context. Prefer PDS4 collection-level identifiers where available, using inspected bundle/collection labels on the GEO node.

candidates.title: MER2 Pancam Calibrated Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Primary calibrated Pancam images used to document Home Plate silica outcrop morphology/albedo/color and map the spatial/stratigraphic relations used in the sinter interpretation.

candidates.title: MER2 Microscopic Imager Calibrated Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Close-up MI images support identification of fine-scale textures (lamination, nodular/digitate surfaces, coatings) that are diagnostic in comparing to terrestrial hot-spring sinter fabrics.

candidates.title: MER2 Mini-TES Calibrated Radiance Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Mini-TES calibrated radiance spectra were used to identify opaline silica spectral signatures at the Home Plate silica outcrops, a key observation for a sinter/hydrothermal precipitation origin.

candidates.title: MER2 Mini-TES Derived Emissivity Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Derived emissivity products are commonly used for mineral identification/mixture analysis; supports the opaline silica (sinter) interpretation compared to alteration/leaching alternatives.

candidates.title: Mini-TES EDC Averaged Mirror-Dust Corrected Image Cube Collection
candidates.reasoning: Peer-reviewed, mirror-dust-corrected Mini-TES emissivity products (Ruff PDART) frequently used for refined silica detections/interpretations around Home Plate, reducing dust/mirror artifacts.

candidates.title: MER APXS Oxide Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: APXS-derived oxide abundances quantify extremely high SiO2 and low concentrations of other oxides at the silica outcrops—chemical evidence consistent with opaline-silica sinter precipitation.

candidates.title: MER2 APXS Xray Spectra (Partially Processed) Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Underlying APXS spectra (partially processed) for re-deriving/validating the silica-rich compositions reported for the Home Plate silica targets.

candidates.title: MER2 MB Summed Spectra Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Moessbauer summed spectra provide supporting mineral/iron-phase context for altered materials near Home Plate; used in many Spirit Home Plate geochemical/mineralogical studies alongside silica detections.

candidates.title: MER2 Pancam Calibrated Data Collection (Derived IOF)
candidates.reasoning: Derived I/F (reflectance) Pancam products enable quantitative spectral/photometric comparisons of the bright silica outcrops versus surrounding materials, supporting composition/texture interpretation.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Geosciences node at https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/.

Directory layout:
  mission/ → dataset_or_bundle/ → volume/ (PDS3) or sub-collections (PDS4)

Top-level mission directories: m2020, insight, msl, mro, mer, mex, ody, phx, mgs, mpf, viking, mariner, mars, mgn, premgn, venus, messenger, grail, clps, lunar, lro, earth, lab, near.

Common mission/instrument abbreviations:
  Mars Express = MEX (instruments: HRSC, OMEGA, MARSIS, PFS, SPICAM, MaRS)
  Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter = MRO (instruments: HiRISE, CTX, CRISM, SHARAD, MCS)
  Mars Science Laboratory / Curiosity = MSL (instruments: ChemCam, APXS, CheMin, SAM, Mastcam, MAHLI, DAN)
  Mars 2020 / Perseverance = M2020 (instruments: PIXL, SHERLOC, Mastcam-Z, SuperCam, RIMFAX)
  Mars Exploration Rovers (Spirit=MER2, Opportunity=MER1) (instruments: Pancam, Mini-TES/MTES, APXS, MB, MI)
  Mars Global Surveyor = MGS (instruments: MOC, MOLA, TES, MAG)
  Mars Odyssey = ODY (instruments: THEMIS, GRS, NS)
  Phoenix = PHX (instruments: TEGA, MECA, SSI, OM, RAC)
  Viking = VL1/VL2/VO1/VO2 (instruments: camera, IRTM, MAWD)
  MESSENGER = MESS (instruments: MDIS, GRNS, XRS, MLA, MASCS)
  Magellan = MGN (instruments: SAR, altimetry, radiometry, emissivity)
  LRO (instruments: LOLA, Diviner, LROC, Mini-RF, LAMP)
  GRAIL (instruments: LGRS)
  NEAR (instruments: NLR, MSI, XGRS, MAG)

PDS3 vs PDS4: both formats co-exist on the same node. PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style identifiers; PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`.

Bundle vs collection (PDS4): a bundle directory holds the bundle label plus sub-directories containing collection labels. `inspect_with_collections` returns both levels.

Extracting dataset_id from labels:
  - PDS3: VOLUME.DATA_SET_ID
  - PDS4 bundle: Identification_Area.logical_identifier (in `labels`)
  - PDS4 collection: Identification_Area.logical_identifier (in `collections`)

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires you to return dataset candidates. Queries come from published scientific papers and always imply specific PDS datasets that the researchers used. NEVER dismiss a query as 'interpretation', 'conceptual', or 'not a dataset question'. Even if the question sounds abstract or science-focused, your job is to identify which PDS datasets would be needed to answer it. Always return at least one candidate.

  2. When search results return a high-scoring match (score >= 80), strongly prefer it as a candidate. Do NOT ignore high-score results in favor of lower-scoring or unrelated datasets.

  3. Prefer CALIBRATED and REDUCED data products over raw/EDR data when the query implies scientific analysis. Scientists typically use calibrated (RDR, CDR) or derived (DDR) products. For MER instruments, prefer the PDS4 calibrated collections (e.g. mer2_pancam_sci_calibrated2, mer2_mi_sci_calibrated, mer2_mtes_calibrated_radiance) over raw EDR data. Search for both 'calibrated' and the instrument name.

  4. For PDS4 datasets, always drill into collections. When you find a PDS4 bundle (urn-nasa-pds-*), use inspect_with_collections to extract collection-level logical_identifiers (e.g. urn:nasa:pds:bundle:data). Return the COLLECTION-level LID when a :data or :calibrated collection exists, not just the bundle LID.

  5. When both PDS3 and PDS4 versions of a dataset exist, prefer the PDS4 collection-level identifier.

Approach:
  - Queries are science-level questions from published papers. Before searching, infer which mission(s) and instrument(s) are relevant using the abbreviation table above, then search holdings with those terms.
  - Search for EACH relevant instrument separately (e.g. 'MEX HRSC DTM', 'MER2 PANCAM', 'MER2 MI'). Do not combine unrelated instruments in one query.
  - After searching holdings, also browse the mission directory for PDS4 bundles (urn-nasa-pds-*) that may contain calibrated versions.
  - If holdings search returns nothing useful, browse the relevant mission directory (e.g. `mro/`) and inspect promising datasets from there.

Constraints:
  - Stay under ~15 tool calls per query.
  - Only return paths you've actually inspected — never guess.
  - You MUST return at least one candidate for every query. An empty candidate list is only acceptable if you have exhausted all search and browse strategies and truly found nothing relevant.
What observations of the Home Plate silica outcrops indicated it was a sinter deposited by hot spring or geyser activity?

Prefer PDS4 collection-level identifiers where available, using inspected bundle/collection labels on the GEO node.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer2_pancam_sci_calibrated2:data","path":"mer/mer2-m-pancam-3-radcal-sci-v2/mer2pc_1002/","title":"MER2 Pancam Calibrated Data Collection","mission":"mer","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Primary calibrated Pancam images used to document Home Plate silica outcrop morphology/albedo/color and map the spatial/stratigraphic relations used in the sinter interpretation."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer2_mi_sci_calibrated:data","path":"mer/mer2-m-mi-3-rdr-sci-v1/mer2mi_1xxx/","title":"MER2 Microscopic Imager Calibrated Data Collection","mission":"mer","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Close-up MI images support identification of fine-scale textures (lamination, nodular/digitate surfaces, coatings) that are diagnostic in comparing to terrestrial hot-spring sinter fabrics."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer2_mtes_calibrated_radiance:data","path":"mer/mer2-m-mtes-3-rdr-v1/mer2mt_1xxx/","title":"MER2 Mini-TES Calibrated Radiance Data Collection","mission":"mer","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Mini-TES calibrated radiance spectra were used to identify opaline silica spectral signatures at the Home Plate silica outcrops, a key observation for a sinter/hydrothermal precipitation origin."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer2_mtes_derived_emissivity:data","path":"mer/mer2-m-mtes-4-emr-v1/mer2mt_3xxx/","title":"MER2 Mini-TES Derived Emissivity Data Collection","mission":"mer","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Derived emissivity products are commonly used for mineral identification/mixture analysis; supports the opaline silica (sinter) interpretation compared to alteration/leaching alternatives."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:ruff_pdart14_mtes:data_mtes_edc","path":"mer/urn-nasa-pds-ruff_pdart14_mtes/","title":"Mini-TES EDC Averaged Mirror-Dust Corrected Image Cube Collection","mission":"mer","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Peer-reviewed, mirror-dust-corrected Mini-TES emissivity products (Ruff PDART) frequently used for refined silica detections/interpretations around Home Plate, reducing dust/mirror artifacts."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer_apxs_derived:data","path":"mer/mer1_mer2-m-apxs-5-oxide-sci-v1/merap_2xxx/","title":"MER APXS Oxide Data Collection","mission":"mer","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"APXS-derived oxide abundances quantify extremely high SiO2 and low concentrations of other oxides at the silica outcrops—chemical evidence consistent with opaline-silica sinter precipitation."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer2_apxs_pp:data","path":"mer/mer2-m-apxs-2-xrayspec-sci-v1/mer2ap_1xxx/","title":"MER2 APXS Xray Spectra (Partially Processed) Data Collection","mission":"mer","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Underlying APXS spectra (partially processed) for re-deriving/validating the silica-rich compositions reported for the Home Plate silica targets."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer2_mb_pp:data","path":"mer/mer2-m-mb-4-sumspec-sci-v1/mer2mb_1xxx/","title":"MER2 MB Summed Spectra Data Collection","mission":"mer","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Moessbauer summed spectra provide supporting mineral/iron-phase context for altered materials near Home Plate; used in many Spirit Home Plate geochemical/mineralogical studies alongside silica detections."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer2_pancam_sci_derived_iof:data","path":"mer/mer2-m-pancam-3-iofcal-sci-v2/mer2pc_3002/","title":"MER2 Pancam Calibrated Data Collection (Derived IOF)","mission":"mer","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Derived I/F (reflectance) Pancam products enable quantitative spectral/photometric comparisons of the bright silica outcrops versus surrounding materials, supporting composition/texture interpretation."}]}
